publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST), United States
verificationNote: NIST SP 800-82 Rev 3 is publicly available at nvlpubs.nist.gov. Content paraphrased for navigability.
scope: Provides guidance for securing Operational Technology (OT) systems including Industrial Control Systems (ICS), SCADA, DCS, PLC-based systems, and other cyber-physical systems. Rev 3 significantly expands coverage beyond ICS to include medical devices, building automation, smart grid, and transportation.
keyChangesRev3: Expanded scope from ICS to all OT systems
Aligned with NIST CSF 2.0 and SP 800-53 Rev 5
Enhanced supply chain risk management guidance
Added cloud, edge computing, and IIoT considerations
Expanded coverage of OT network architecture patterns
New guidance on OT incident response
Updated threat landscape including ransomware and nation-state TTPs
relationToIEC62443: NIST SP 800-82 and IEC 62443 are complementary. 800-82 provides the US federal government framework reference and extensive architecture guidance; IEC 62443 provides the internationally recognised certification framework with SL-based requirements. Malaysian NCII operators should use IEC 62443 as the primary standard with 800-82 as supplementary architecture guidance.

chapters.title: Introduction
chapters.keyContent: OT system types, components, and architectures. Differences between OT and IT security. Key concepts: availability-first, physical consequences, legacy constraints, real-time requirements.

chapters.title: Overview of OT
chapters.keyContent: SCADA, DCS, PLC systems, Remote I/O, safety systems, building automation, medical device systems, smart grid. Component descriptions: RTU, PLC, HMI, historian, engineering workstation, data concentrator.

chapters.title: OT Cybersecurity Program
chapters.keyContent: OT security program structure: risk management, policies, asset management, workforce development, incident response, supply chain. Roles and responsibilities: CISO, OT security engineer, operations.

chapters.title: OT Security Architecture
chapters.keyContent: Network zone segmentation, Industrial DMZ, reference architectures (single site, enterprise, cloud-connected). Purdue model evolution. Remote access architecture. Cloud-connected OT patterns.

chapters.title: Managing Risk
chapters.keyContent: OT-specific risk assessment methodology. Asset-based risk model. Threat identification, vulnerability assessment, consequence analysis. Risk treatment options in OT context.

chapters.title: Applying Security Controls
chapters.keyContent: SP 800-53 control families applied to OT context. OT-specific control considerations per family. Compensating controls for legacy OT. Control testing approaches for OT.
